subject: Re: Warp 1.09 - Error while processing CTFs
body:
Hi Ivan,

I tried replicating this on a few test shots, and there seems to be a bug 
where a negative defocus input (your -5 µm) produces this error. You should 
input it as a positive value, but you might need to delete some or all of 
the Warp output files before Warp will run normally again.

/Andreas

onsdag den 16. marts 2022 kl. 17.29.28 UTC+1 skrev Ivan Zumeta:

> Hi everyone,
>
> We are new to WARP, and we have run into a problem while making the CTF 
> correction of our movies. 
>
> When we started doing motion correction, Warp was doing fine, but when we 
> tried to do the CTF, Warp started deselecting all the files. Trying to do a 
> single CTF (in the Fourier tab) gives an error (see image attached) 
>
> We tried playing with the parameters, but it always gives the same error. 
> For reference, we want to use (and have used) the following CTF parameters:
>
> Window: 512 px
> Range: 30-5 A
> Voltage: 300kV
> Cs: 2.7 mm
> Amplitude: 0.1
> Defocus: 0 - -5 um
>
> Kind regards,
>
> Ivan
>
